thesis: AST SpaceMobile is pioneering the first space-based cellular broadband network accessible by standard smartphones. The company priced $1B of 1.625% convertible senior notes due 2034 at a 20% premium ($79.57 conversion price) with capped calls up to $149.20, adding ~$984M (up to $1.13B with shoe) to a June 30 cash balance of ~$2.72B. The launch campaign has slipped to ~45 BlueBird satellites in early 2027 (from late 2026), and use-of-proceeds language signals potential investment or acquisition of a launch provider. The stock has sold off from $133.86 highs to ~$66.31, improving risk/reward for speculative investors, though execution and competitive risks from SpaceX remain acute.

drivers: Successful deployment of ~45 BlueBird satellites by early 2027 enables continuous U.S. coverage
AT&T and Verizon commercial service launches in H2 2026–H1 2027 drive subscriber adoption above 5% of rural customers
International partnerships (Vodafone, Rakuten, stc) generate material revenue by 2027
Government contracts scale to $200M+ annually with 20–30% margins
Revenue grows from $150M–$200M (FY2026) to $2–$3B by 2028 with gross margins expanding to 60%+

base_case.assumptions: ASTS deploys ~45 satellites by early 2027 and achieves continuous coverage. Commercial service launches with mixed adoption—grows steadily but not explosively. SpaceX competition is real but ASTS maintains 30–40% market share through carrier partnerships. Revenue grows to $400–$600M by 2027 and $1.5B by 2029. Company reaches EBITDA breakeven by 2028–2029. Stock trades based on execution with multiple compressing to 50–80x sales as growth matures.

risks: SpaceX Starlink with T-Mobile proves 'good enough' for most users, capturing 70%+ market share
AT&T or Verizon switch allegiance to SpaceX or develop in-house solutions
Satellite launches face further delays or technical issues; constellation incomplete by 2027
Revenue growth stalls below $150M in FY2026; cash burn forces highly dilutive financing
Market loses patience with 'story stocks'; multiple compression to 30x sales or lower

catalysts.event: Launch campaign targeting ~45 BlueBird satellites (slipped from late 2026)
Q2 2026 earnings — revenue acceleration vs. Q1 and updated launch timeline are critical
$1B convertible notes priced; 1.625% coupon, $79.57 conversion price, capped calls up to $149.20
Government contract awards (DoD, Space Development Agency, Golden Dome)
Potential acquisition or partnership with a launch provider (ULA, Firefly, Relativity, Stoke)

key_risks: SpaceX Starlink competition with deeper pockets, lower launch costs, and T-Mobile partnership
BlueBird 7 write-off of $155M–$160M raises manufacturing and execution risks
Q1 2026 EPS miss (-$0.66 vs. -$0.21 est.) shows larger-than-expected losses
Heavy customer concentration with AT&T and Verizon; loss of either partner would collapse revenue
Need for 5–7x revenue growth to reach profitability; FY2026 guidance of $150M–$200M is still far from breakeven
$1B convertible note overhang creates potential future dilution, though capped calls and 20% premium limit economic dilution unless stock is above $80
Launch campaign delay to early 2027 pushes back commercialization timeline and may strain carrier confidence
Use-of-proceeds hints at acquisition/investment in a launch provider; integration risk and capital allocation uncertainty
High cash burn rate (~$150M+ per quarter normalized) requires disciplined execution
